logical_connection.title: Twin Prime Conjecture via Power Law Distribution

premises.name: Statement 8 (Empirical)
premises.statement: Twin prime gap distribution follows power law

logical_flow: 2. Twin prime gaps follow power law distribution (Statement 8)
3. Power law with exponent -ln(σ₂) has infinite support
4. Therefore, infinitely many twin primes exist

lemmas.name: twin_prime_counting_function
lemmas.statement: Define π₂(x) = number of twin prime pairs ≤ x
lemmas.formalization: π₂(x) = |{p ≤ x : p and p+2 both prime}|

lemmas.name: twin_prime_gap_power_law
lemmas.statement: Twin prime gap frequency follows power law
lemmas.formalization: f(g) = C · g^(-ln σ₂) for g ≥ g_min
challenges: Prove the power law is exact, not approximate
Determine constants C and g_min analytically
Prove this holds for all scales, not just up to 10⁵

lemmas.name: power_normalization
lemmas.statement: Power law normalization sum diverges for exponent < 1

lemmas.name: divergence_implies_infinitude
lemmas.statement: If sum of twin prime gaps diverges, there are infinitely many twin primes
lemmas.proof_strategy: Contrapositive: if finite number, sum converges

gap_1.name: Exact Power Law
gap_1.approach: Deep analytic number theory, Riemann zeta function

gap_2.name: Constant Determination
gap_2.issue: Constants C and g_min not known analytically
gap_2.requirement: Determine C and g_min from first principles

gap_3.name: Universal Validity
gap_3.issue: Power law observed only up to 10⁵
gap_3.requirement: Prove power law holds for all scales
